Title: Cabinet orders DBKL governance reforms before law review
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Cabinet has directed the Federal Territories Department to implement governance reforms at Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) before considering any amendments to the Federal Capital Act 1960 (Act 190).
Federal Territories Minister in the Prime Minister's Department Hannah Yeoh said the decision followed the Cabinet's review of a feasibility study on proposed amendments to Act 190 conducted by the International Islamic University Malaysia (IIUM) between Dec 1, 2025, and March 31 this year.
The study found that improving DBKL's governance did not necessarily require legislative amendments at the initial stage. Instead, reforms could begin through administrative measures, including strengthening governance practices, enhancing checks and balances, and improving internal institutional coordination.
"Following the Cabinet's decision, the Federal Territories Department and DBKL are now developing a transformation plan to strengthen DBKL's governance and ensure the city administration remains relevant and fit for purpose in the future," she said in a statement today.
Hannah said IIUM, as part of the study, held engagement sessions with Kuala Lumpur member or parliaments on March 2 and DBKL's management on March 9 to gather views on the city hall's administrative structure, decision-making processes, service delivery, enforcement and accountability.
She said the study also examined a proposal by the Prime Minister's Advisory Council (PMAC) to change the legal structure of the Kuala Lumpur mayor from a sole corporation to a corporate body through the establishment of a Supreme Council, as well as a Private Member's Bill tabled by several Kuala Lumpur MPs under Standing Order 49 of the Dewan Rakyat.
However, Hannah said the study concluded that any amendments to Act 190 must be approached with caution as they involve the highest level of governance, including the Yang di-Pertuan Agong and the Sultan of Selangor, and are closely linked to the historical and constitutional framework of the Federal Constitution.
"In this context, the identified improvements should first be implemented through internal administrative reforms, such as internal directives, guidelines, operational protocols and institutional coordination mechanisms, before any amendments to the statutory framework are considered," she said.
The study also concluded that there is currently no need to introduce a city councillor system within DBKL, as existing governance structures can be strengthened without creating an additional layer that could lead to overlapping functions and unclear accountability.
"It recommended strengthening the governance framework of the DBKL Advisory Board, including setting clearer criteria for the appointment of professionals and representatives from non-governmental organisations (NGOs).
"It also proposed reinforcing the role of Kuala Lumpur MPs as a democratic oversight institution and public representative channel without granting them direct administrative powers," Hannah said.
According to her, the study found that most implementation issues stemmed from the lack of clear and consistent governance guidelines and procedures with sufficient legal authority, rather than shortcomings in existing legislation.
She added that the study reaffirmed that Kuala Lumpur's administration under the sole corporation model, as provided for under Section 5(1) of Act 190, remains consistent with the original legislative intent, and that altering the structure could undermine the capital city's unique governance framework.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia retains 0.05 blood alcohol limit as police study tougher drink-driving laws
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia will retain its 0.05 per cent blood alcohol concentration (BAC) limit, while police study tougher penalties for drink- and drug-driving offences.
Bukit Aman Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department (TEID) director Datuk Seri Muhammed Hasbullah Ali said the existing BAC threshold remained in line with indicators established by the Health Ministry.
"Our limit is 0.05 per cent and we follow the standards and indicators determined under Malaysian law," he said during a press conference here today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mother elephant stays by calf's body after crash kills calf, injures driver
Article:
MERSING: A 31-year-old motorist was injured after his car collided with an elephant calf before plunging into a five-metre-deep ravine along Jalan Felda Nitar near Kampung Pengkalan Bukit Asli here early today.
The elephant calf died in the crash, while an adult elephant, believed to be its mother, remained at the site, disrupting traffic in the area.
Johor Health and Environment Committee chairman Ling Tian Soon said the Johor Elephant Action Unit was deployed to manage the situation and ensure public safety.
He added that the calf was later buried and the mother elephant was fitted with a monitoring collar before being released back into the jungle once the operation was concluded.
"The operation has been completed and the situation is now under control," he said.
A firefighter at the scene told the New Straits Times that the calf died from its injuries.
Mersing Fire and Rescue station chief Mohd Alias Hussin said the department received an emergency call at 2.28am.
Nine personnel were dispatched to the scene, located about 27km from the station, arriving at 3.03am.
"The victim was travelling towards Felda Nitar in Kahang, Kluang, when his car hit the calf before veering off the road and plunging into a ravine," he said.
Alias said the driver, who was trapped inside the wreckage, was rescued by firefighters and sustained leg injuries. He was taken to Mersing Hospital for treatment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pahang Op Hawk: 333 arrested, drugs worth over RM500k seized
Article:
KUANTAN: Drugs, cash and vehicles worth more than half a million ringgit were seized and 333 people arrested in a three-day statewide anti-drug operation in Pahang targeting hotspots across all 11 districts.
The crackdown, known as Op Hawk, was conducted by the Pahang Narcotics Crime Investigation Department.
The arrests involved individuals aged between 19 and 65, who were detained for drug distribution, possession and drug use offences.
Pahang acting police chief Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said various drugs, including ketum juice worth about RM763,000, along with cash and vehicles amounting to RM79,600, were seized.
"The operation, carried out in all 11 districts and concluded yesterday, targeted Felda schemes, makeshift drug dens and other identified drug hotspots.
"Police conducted 167 raids and 393 inspections on houses, vehicles and individuals. Of the 333 arrested, 73 were detained for drug distribution, 85 for drug possession and 175 tested positive for drugs," he said in a statement today.
Azry Akmar said the seized drugs included heroin, methamphetamine (syabu), cannabis (ganja), Yaba pills (also known as pil kuda), Erimin 5, ketamine, cocaine, MDMA (methylenedioxymethamphetamine), as well as ketum water and leaves.
He said members of the public with information on drug-related activities can contact the Pahang police contingent control centre at 09-505 1999/2022.
Op Hawk is a special operation targeting drug hotspots across Pahang and is conducted regularly to curb drug-related and other illegal activities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Nothing left except death': Myanmar families grieve huge war toll
Article:
RANKS of bereaved Myanmar families bow in silent grief to pray for their dead, some of the latest victims in a years-long conflict now estimated to have claimed 100,000 lives.
Clasping their hands at the monastery hall in central Magway region, they offer alms for the deceased: small cash notes, rice and curry – all they can spare while living through a civil war.
"How many more young people will have to die?" asked Soe Gyi, recounting how his nephew was killed in combat after he quit training as a monk to fight for pro-democracy rebels.
"Only the elderly and the very young children are left," said the 49-year-old last month, speaking under a pseudonym to protect his security, like all others in this story.
"The generation in between has been cut down."
Monitor organisation Armed Conflict Location & Event Data (ACLED) said on Wednesday that the number of conflict-related fatalities across all sides since a 2021 coup now stands at 100,114.
ACLED tallies media reports of violence. There is no official verified figure, and estimates vary widely.
But all around Magway's Myit Chay area – on the banks of the Irrawaddy river bisecting Myanmar – the emotional toll is unquestionable.
Thaung Sein said her civilian son was killed as their family was displaced by a recent military offensive.
"If our younger generation of children can survive until they grow up, there might still be a chance for a better future," said the 45-year-old from her temporary shelter in the jungle.
"But if they cannot even make it that far and things continue like this, there will be nothing left except death."
Her son's body was discovered burned and pierced with puncture wounds in a bleak landscape of charred villages, she said.
Rescuers took pictures of his remains but refused to show her, fearing she would be overwhelmed at the sight.
"In this war, they are simply killing people indiscriminately," she said. "There is no regard at all for whether someone lives or dies."
AFP has not been able to independently verify the accounts of interviewees in this story.
The civil war began when a 2021 coup deposing the elected government of Aung San Suu Kyi prompted pro-democracy activists to take up arms against the military, which was already fighting long-running conflicts with ethnic minority armies.
Analysts say that after five years, the military is now on the front foot, making advances across the country and signing strategic truces with some ethnic factions.
But rebel groups defiantly patrol Myit Chay: a sign there is still more violence to come.
"Before the coup, our family lived happily and laughed together, sharing whatever we had," recalled 39-year-old Yin Than, whose husband went to war in the name of democracy before being killed in April 2024.
"It wasn't a natural death, he died like a dog," she said.
"Now that he is gone, who are my child and I supposed to rely on?"
Around Myit Chay, another toll from the war is also clearly visible.
The United Nations says 3.7 million people in Myanmar have been internally displaced.
Monasteries where families grieve also serve as temporary shelters where those forced to flee sleep among their bundled belongings.
But it offers little reprieve. "Nothing is going well anywhere," said Yin Than.
"It's not OK at home, and it's not OK in the places we've fled to."
* The article is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 121 commercial vehicle drivers held in anti-drug crackdown
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police arrested 121 commercial vehicle drivers for allegedly driving under the influence of drugs during a nationwide enforcement operation over the past three months, highlighting concerns over impaired driving among operators of heavy vehicles.
Bukit Aman Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department (TEID) director Datuk Seri Muhammed Hasbullah Ali said the arrests were made during 44 integrated operations conducted between April and June with the cooperation of the Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (NCID) and the National Anti-Drugs Agency (AADK).
He said 627 enforcement personnel — comprising 273 TEID officers, 31 NCID officers and 323 AADK officers — carried out 713 urine screening tests on commercial vehicle drivers nationwide.
Of the 135 people arrested, 121 were lorry drivers, while the remaining 14 drove other categories of commercial vehicles, meaning nearly 90 per cent of those detained were lorry drivers.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chow: Penang deserves national revenue share that matches its economic weight
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang has renewed calls for a fairer share of federal revenue after the prime minister acknowledged the state contributes more in taxes than it receives in development spending.
Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the prime minister's statement in the Dewan Rakyat validated Penang's longstanding position that the state deserved development allocations commensurate with its contribution to the national economy.
He said the state was not seeking special treatment but a fair distribution of returns based on its actual economic contribution.
"As one of the nation's key manufacturing, trade, technology and innovation hubs, Penang requires continued investment in strategic infrastructure to maintain Malaysia's global competitiveness," he said in a statement today.
Chow also welcomed the federal government's commitment since 2023 in approving and funding major infrastructure projects in the state, including the Mutiara Light Rail Transit (LRT) line, the expansion of Penang International Airport, the Juru-Sungai Dua elevated highway traffic dispersal project, flood mitigation works and other strategic development initiatives.
He commended the prime minister for publicly disclosing federal revenue collection and expenditure figures in Parliament, describing the move as a reflection of the Madani government's commitment to transparency, accountability and good governance.
Citing the state's economic performance, Chow said Penang recorded RM63.96 billion in approved investments in 2025, comprising RM25.10 billion in foreign direct investment and RM38.85 billion in domestic direct investment.
He said Penang also remained Malaysia's leading exporting state, with exports valued at RM573.91 billion in 2025, accounting for 35.7 per cent of the country's total exports.
Chow said these achievements demonstrated that investments channelled into Penang generated substantial returns for the national economy through higher exports, quality employment, technology transfer and increased federal tax revenue.
He also urged the federal government to prioritise efforts to realise the proposed Penang International Financial Centre, saying it would complement the state's high-technology manufacturing base by strengthening the financial and high-value services sectors.
Chow said the state government will continue working with Putrajaya to ensure Penang remains a key engine of Malaysia's economic growth while pursuing a more equitable and strategic distribution of development resources.
Yesterday, Datuk Seri
Anwar Ibrahim
told the Dewan Rakyat that nearly every state except Selangor and Penang, received federal allocations that exceeded the tax revenue it contributes.
He said Selangor and Penang collected high tax revenues because of their strategic economic positions, but federal expenditure there was comparatively lower.
Penang contributed RM10.7 billion in taxes and received RM7.9 billion in federal spending.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kim Dotcom loses latest appeal against extradition to US from New Zealand
Article:
SYDNEY: Kim Dotcom lost his latest appeal on Wednesday to avoid extradition to the United States, where he is facing criminal charges relating to the defunct file-sharing website Megaupload that he founded.
German-born Dotcom, who has New Zealand residency, has been fighting extradition to the United States since 2012 following an FBI-ordered raid on his Auckland mansion.
Dotcom, who is on bail, was appealing a 2024 decision by New Zealand that he be extradited to the US. He was 50 that year.
New Zealand's Court of Appeal found on Wednesday there was no legal basis to block his extradition to the US, according to a copy of the judgment.
Dotcom has one final route to appeal the ruling, through the country's top court, the Supreme Court.
Dotcom did not immediately respond to a request for comment via his lawyer on Wednesday.
US authorities say Dotcom and three other Megaupload executives cost film studios and record companies more than US$500 million by encouraging paying users to store and share copyrighted material, which generated more than US$175 million in revenue for the website.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Volkswagen cuts are 'wake-up call' for European industry
Article:
BERLIN: Volkswagen's reported plans to drastically ramp up cuts are a "wake-up call" for the European automotive industry as Chinese carmakers target a higher market share, BYD's special advisor for the region said on Wednesday.
"It's the first real wake-up call for the European industry," Alfredo Altavilla, BYD's special advisor for the European market, told the Reuters Automotive Europe conference in Frankfurt.
He expressed doubts over the competitiveness of German manufacturing sites as BYD looks to boost its production footprint in Europe with a brownfield investment. Spain and France are candidates and a final decision is close, he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tesla's June registrations rise in France, Sweden, Denmark as Europe recovery continues
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: New registrations of Tesla vehicles rose across several European markets in June, extending a recent recovery in the US electric vehicle maker's regional sales.
Tesla registrations, a proxy for sales, more than doubled in France and increased 39 per cent in Denmark, and 56 per cent in Sweden, according to year-on-year data released on Wednesday by French car body PFA, Denmark's bilstatistik.dk and Mobility Sweden.
Tesla is expected to report a 5.0 per cent rise in second-quarter vehicle deliveries, with much of the growth likely driven by stronger demand in Europe, where higher fuel prices have encouraged consumers to switch to battery-electric vehicles.
Registrations of battery-electric vehicles in Europe rose 39.1 per cent in May, according to the European Automobile Manufacturers' Association (ACEA).
Norway bucked the trend in June with a 43 per cent fall in new Tesla registrations from a year earlier, data from compiler OFV showed.
Britain and Germany, Europe's two largest car markets, are due to release June registration data later this week.
Tesla, the world's most valuable automaker by market capitalisation, lost almost half of its European market share in 2025 due to a combination of growing competition, especially from Chinese brands, its lack of new models and a reaction to CEO Elon Musk's political stance.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: JS-SEZ masterplan, investment blueprint to be launched by Q4: Akmal Nasrullah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Johor–Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) masterplan and investment blueprint is expected to be launched by the fourth quarter of 2026, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.
He said the document is already in place, with government agencies currently working to deliver investments that have been pledged to the economic zone.
"I think we can further explore the details of the document when the time comes," he told a press conference after launching the JS-SEZ Executive Forum 2026 here, today.
The blueprint will serve as a comprehensive roadmap, outlining the strategic, systematic and collaborative framework for the next phase of cross-border economic development.
Iskandar Regional Development Authority federal commissioner and director of corporate management and finance, Nor Hisham Mohd Yusof, said the blueprint, developed in collaboration with the Economy Ministry, will elaborate on key investment focus areas, priority sectors, infrastructure enhancements and talent ecosystem development.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mara Inc, BHPetrol roll out solar at 50 service stations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Mara Incorporated Sdn Bhd (Mara Inc) and Boustead Petroleum Marketing Sdn Bhd (BHPetrol) have launched a phased solar photovoltaic (PV) installation project across 50 service stations in Klang Valley and Peninsular Malaysia.
The initiative supports Malaysia's energy transition agenda and aims to expand renewable energy adoption within the petroleum retail sector.
Mara Inc chief executive officer Faizul Idzhar Ahmad Yazi said the project aligns with the company's long-term green investment strategy and delivers measurable environmental benefits.
"The 1.4 megawatt-peak solar PV system will contribute significantly towards a low-carbon economy and more sustainable business practices, with an estimated reduction of about 1,347 tonnes of carbon dioxide annually," he said.
He added that the rollout follows a structured tender process and all costs are determined through market-based pricing.
"We go through a proper competitive tender process with multiple bidders. We analyse the unit rate, and that becomes our cost," he said.
The project was launched at the BHPetrol Jalan Genting Klang station here today by Mara director-general Datuk Zulfikri Osman.
Mara Inc is also expanding its renewable energy portfolio beyond BHPetrol, including solar installations at Mara educational institutions and large-scale solar projects exceeding 100 megawatts in the east coast region.
"The solar initiative has strong long-term potential because electricity cost savings are typically fixed over 10 to 20 years, making it very attractive for commercial premises," Faizul said.
"We are also looking to expand collaboration with other government-linked and institutional partners as demand for solar deployment continues to grow nationwide," he added.
As of July 1, 16 BHPetrol stations have already been equipped with solar PV systems, implemented in collaboration with Mara Inc and other agencies, with more expected to come online by year-end.
BHPetrol acting chief executive officer Fardzi Shah Razali said the initiative is part of efforts to reduce operating costs while strengthening the company's sustainability agenda.
"This initiative is not only about sustainability, but also about reducing operating costs across our network," he said.
BHPetrol currently operates more than 420 service stations nationwide, with installations carried out in phases based on technical suitability rather than location.
"We are not focusing only on Klang Valley. We select stations based on suitability because solar requires open space and good sunlight exposure. Some urban stations are not suitable due to surrounding high-rise buildings," he said.
Fardzi said the programme supports Malaysia's National Energy Transition Roadmap, which encourages greater adoption of renewable energy across industries.
"This initiative is aligned with the NETR, and it reflects how industry players like us are contributing towards the country's low-carbon aspirations," he said.
He added that the programme is a long-term initiative expected to run for at least 10 years, reflecting the nature of renewable energy investments.
Fardzi said station operators are expected to benefit from lower electricity bills with solar systems capable of reducing usage by at least 10 per cent.
"For example, if a station's monthly electricity bill is RM6,000, it can be reduced by at least RM600," he said.
The current phase covers 50 stations under the partnership with future expansion dependent on performance and operational outcomes before scaling further across the network, he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US firm Vertiv expects Johor plant to reach technical capacity by Q3 2027
Article:
JOHOR BARU: US-based digital infrastructure company Vertiv Group expects its Johor manufacturing facility to gradually ramp up production, with about 15 to 20 per cent of its production lines currently in operation.
Vertiv's Asia vice president, operations and service operations, Andrew Whall, said the newly launched factory is expected to approach its technical production capacity by the end of the second quarter or early third quarter of 2027 (3Q 2027), supported by the introduction of new products.
"We've probably got about 15 to 20 per cent of the facility producing right now. I am expecting (technical production) probably towards 2Q or early 3Q 2027," he told reporters after the launch of Vertiv's Johor manufacturing facility today.
Whall said the facility has been fully operational since 1Q of this year, with shipments of its XDU (eXtreme Density Unit) product line already underway.
He said that the facility is currently in its first phase of development, and preparations for the second and third phases are already in motion to expand production capacity and broaden its product portfolio.
"The (current) factory floor is our phase one. Phase two is already in motion, with additional production lines for new liquid-cooling products as well as SmartRun capacity.
"We're already in the planning stages for phase three, which will introduce new and different products onto the production floor," he said.
Whall said the production floor is expected to be fully occupied with additional product lines by mid-next year, enabling the facility to serve both the Malaysian and regional markets, including Australia and South Korea, and to create up to 500 skilled jobs in the region.
When asked why Vertiv chose Johor as its regional manufacturing hub, Vertiv's Asia vice president and general manager, Paul Churchill, said the decision followed a comprehensive evaluation of locations across Asia, particularly Southeast Asia, driven by growing customer demand and the need for local manufacturing capacity.
"We could see there was greater demand and a stronger requirement to have local capacity. We engaged external consultants to help us with that and carried out an evaluation throughout Asia, predominantly Southeast Asia.
"We had a set of criteria that needed to be met, and Malaysia was successful in meeting those criteria. It was really about connectivity, proximity to the marketplace, the work being done here in Johor, education levels, English language capability, and having a government agency such as the Malaysian Investment Development Authority (MIDA) to help guide us through the process," he said.
In a separate statement, Vertiv said the Johor facility supports end-to-end manufacturing, assembly, and testing for advanced thermal and power infrastructure, enabling the delivery of high-density solutions with validated performance to help reduce deployment risk and accelerate time to capacity for customers across enterprise, cloud, and colocation segments.
It will manufacture large-scale thermal management, power and infrastructure solutions for artificial intelligence (AI) and traditional applications, including Vertiv CoolChip coolant distribution units (CDUs), Vertiv Power Module and Power Skid prefabricated power systems, as well as the Vertiv SmartRun integrated overhead infrastructure system.
Located in Senai, Johor, the approximately 236,000-square-foot facility is part of Vertiv's expanded manufacturing footprint in Asia to support growing demand for AI and high-density computing infrastructure across Southeast Asia, North Asia, Australia and New Zealand.
Also present at the launch of the Johor factory were the chief executive officer of Invest Johor, Natazha Hariss, and MIDA Johor director Mohamad Reduan Mohd Zabri.
Headquartered in Ohio, USA, Vertiv does business in more than 130 countries and has 30 manufacturing facilities worldwide.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Father of 2 jailed, caned for restaurant break-in
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: An unemployed man was today sentenced by the Sessions Court to three years in jail and one stroke of the rotan, six months after he broke into a restaurant and made away with cash and a mobile phone.
Dressed in an orange lock-up outfit in the dock, M. Yugendran, 33, who is a father of two, pleaded guilty to breaking into an Indian restaurant in Titiwangsa Sentral after cutting off its padlock at 10am on Jan 23.
He then pried open the cash register and stole RM500 in cash, along with a mobile phone from the cashier's counter.
Yugendran also admitted that he was the person captured inside the premises closed circuit television camera recording.
The restaurant's 51-year-old owner discovered the break-in and lodged a police report, estimating her losses at RM1,000.
The charge framed under Section 457 of the Penal Code carries a jail term between five and 14 years and a fine.
Judge Dr Azrol Abdullah ordered Yugendran to serve the jail term from his arrest on June 22.
In mitigation, Yugendran, who was unrepresented, pleaded for leniency, telling the court that he is a divorcee and that his children, aged 10 and 9, are being cared for by his mother.
He also told the judge he was unemployed before his arrest.
However, deputy public prosecutor Mohamad Ikhwan Mohd Nasir asked the court to impose a proportionate sentence to deter the recurrence of similar offences.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Binastra bags RM491.2mil contract to build Cheras data centre
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Binastra Corp Bhd's unit Binastra Builders Sdn Bhd has bagged a RM491.2 million contract from Exsim Sri Permai Sdn Bhd to build a seven-storey data centre in Cheras.
The contract covers the design, construction and infrastructure works for the data centre, including the main building and related facilities.
Construction is scheduled to begin on July 1 this year with completion targeted within 18 months.
The latest award marks Binastra's seventh data centre project, bringing the toal value of its data centre contracts to about RM2.4 billion.
The project represents Phase 2 of the Cheras data centre development, following the Phase 1 contract awarded in February, which is currently under construction.
Managing director Datuk Jackson Tan Kak Seng said Malaysia's position as a regional data centre hub continues to create strong opportunities, supported by sustained investments from global technology companies to meet growing demand for artificial intelligence and cloud computing infrastructure.
"As we continue to expand our construction capabilities and profile, we remain well-positioned to capture further opportunities through a selective approach focused on high-value data centre and green-related projects," he said in a statement.
The latest contract raises Binastra's new contract wins for the financial year 2027 to RM1.3 billion, while its outstanding order book has grown to RM6.8 billion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Majestic Gen unveils new leadership team for next growth phase
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Majestic Gen Sdn Bhd has appointed Wong Yik Fong as chief executive officer (CEO) effective July 1, as part of a leadership restructuring aimed at supporting the property developer's next phase of growth and expansion.
The company also said that Datuk Hoo Kim See will assume the role of chairman and director, while Ta Wee Dher has been appointed as group managing director.
In a statement, Majestic Gen said the new leadership structure is intended to strengthen governance, succession planning and operational execution as it expands its development portfolio across residential, commercial, industrial and integrated projects.
Hoo will focus on board-level oversight, corporate governance and long-term strategy, while Ta will lead business growth initiatives, including land acquisitions, development planning, strategic partnerships and project pipeline expansion.
Wong will oversee the group's daily operations, including project delivery, sales performance, stakeholder management and organisational development.
The company said Wong brings more than two decades of experience in civil engineering, project management and township development. He previously served as chief operating officer at Trinity Group and held senior positions at Gamuda Land, where he was involved in major township developments, including Gamuda Gardens and Gamuda Cove.
Wong said his priority will be to strengthen operational execution, improve project delivery and align teams to support the group's growth plans.
Since the company's rebranding in February 2024, it has expanded its market presence and development pipeline, launching three projects with a combined gross development value (GDV) of RM971.6 million in 2025.
This year it targets to launch four projects worth RM370.5 million.
Majestic Gen said it will continue focusing on landbank expansion, new business ventures and enhancing project delivery as it seeks to strengthen its position in Malaysia's property development sector.
On the company's next growth phase, Ta said the company will focus on expanding its landbank, pursuing strategic opportunities and strengthening its project pipeline while maintaining a disciplined approach to growth.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan not among states with highest crime rates, says deputy MB
Article:
KOTA BARU: Kelantan is not among the states with the highest crime rates in the country, Deputy Menteri Besar Datuk Dr Mohamed Fadzli Hassan said, citing official government statistics to dispute claims that the state is grappling with the nation's worst social problems.
He said data published by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) showed Kelantan recorded 2,296 index crime cases in 2024, with an index crime ratio of 109, significantly lower than Selangor's 11,910 cases.
He said the statistics did not place Kelantan among the states with the highest overall crime rates.
For sexual violence, Fadzli said Kelantan recorded 330 cases in 2024, fewer than Selangor (1,308), Johor (681), Sabah (573), Perak (543) and Penang (388).
He added that the state recorded 147 rape cases, compared with 359 in Selangor, 232 in Sabah and 223 in Johor.
On child sexual crimes, he said Kelantan recorded 128 statutory rape cases, lower than Selangor's 288, Sabah's 207 and Johor's 198.
He added that no cases of child sexual exploitation were recorded in Kelantan last year.
"The DOSM report also showed Kelantan recorded 22 cases of physical abuse involving children, compared with 441 in Selangor and 148 in Johor, while no cases of child abduction or child neglect under guardianship were recorded in the state last year.
"The official data clearly shows that attempts to portray Kelantan as the state with the highest incidence of crime and child sexual offences are inaccurate, incomplete and misleading," he told reporters after the state executive council meeting here today.
Fadzli said the state government viewed seriously recent allegations circulating on social media and raised in Parliament concerning crime and social issues in Kelantan.
He said comments on crime and social ills should be made responsibly, based on complete official data rather than selectively presented information that could create a misleading political narrative.
"The state government regrets the actions of certain individuals, including a minister and a member of parliament, who have misled the public by failing to use official government data and instead relying on unverified sources while speaking in the Dewan Rakyat," he said.
Fadzli was referring to remarks by Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming, who allegedly claimed in a viral video that Kelantan was facing serious social problems, including HIV/AIDS infections, rape, child sexual offences and drug abuse.
He also took issue with remarks by Jelutong MP RSN Rayer during the debate on the Sexual Offences Against Children (Amendment) Bill 2026, saying the lawmaker had cited reports from a news portal instead of official national statistics, creating the impression that Kelantan recorded the highest rate of child sexual crimes.
Yesterday,
a heated exchange erupted
in the Dewan Rakyat after an MP was accused of citing outdated statistics to claim that child sexual crime cases were on the rise in Kelantan.
The shouting match began when RSN Rayer (PH-Jelutong) defended remarks he made on Monday, insisting that reports he had cited, including one published by the New Straits Times, showed an increase in sexual crime cases in Kelantan.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thai woman, partner arrested in RM728k drug bust in Bentong
Article:
KUANTAN: A couple allegedly working as drug couriers for a syndicate over the past three months were arrested in Genting Permai, Bentong, with drugs worth about RM728,000.
Their regular trips to the popular highlands resort in Bentong, however, had not gone unnoticed.
After monitoring the duo, a team from the Pahang police contingent moved in and arrested them at a hotel parking area in Genting Permai at about 5pm on June 29.
The suspects, who were reportedly paid RM5,000 for each trip, were found in possession of an assortment of drugs worth more than half a million ringgit.
Pahang acting police chief Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said the 37-year-old male suspect and his 35-year-old Thai girlfriend had booked a room at the hotel and were about to leave the premises in their multi-purpose vehicle when police moved in.
"Police seized packets containing ketamine, cocaine, methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA), syabu and Erimin 5 pills. Cash amounting to RM1,000 and a gold bracelet were also recovered.
"The couple, who had no previous criminal records, tested positive for methamphetamine. They have been remanded until July 6," he told reporters at a press conference today.
The suspects are being investigated under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 for drug trafficking. Upon conviction, the offence carries the death penalty or life imprisonment, along with mandatory whipping.
Meanwhile, Azry Akmar said investigations revealed that the suspects acted only as transporters, bringing the drugs from the Klang Valley for distribution on the east coast.
"They were paid RM5,000 for transporting the drugs from one location to another, and we believe they have been visiting the highlands regularly. Checks revealed the male suspect has a full-time job as a shop assistant," he said, adding that the seized drugs could supply about 10,000 addicts.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Manufacturing momentum lifts Malaysia export prospects: MARC Ratings
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's export outlook remains supported by improving manufacturing conditions, with the latest Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) readings in China, the US and the euro area continuing to signal expansion.
MARC Ratings Bhd said the US–Iran de-escalation of tensions and continued safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz will remain important determinants of the global trade outlook.
Malaysia's export growth strengthened further to 45.3 per cent in May from 37.3 per cent in April, marking a second consecutive month of double digit expansion.
MARC said the improvement was driven by the global semiconductor upcycle and strong demand for artificial intelligence (AI) related technologies, which lifted electrical and electronics (E&E) exports by 70.5 per cent in May, compared with 46.6 per cent in April.
It added that trade activity continued to benefit from supply chain diversification amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainties.
Meanwhile, headline inflation edged up to 2.0 per cent in May from 1.9 per cent in April, mainly due to firmer price increases in food and non-alcoholic beverages as well as housing and utilities.
However, inflation moderated in the transportation category to 3.8 per cent from 4.1 per cent, while restaurants and accommodation services eased to 2.5 per cent from 2.6 per cent.
MARC expects inflation to remain broadly in line with its 2026 forecast of 2.1 per cent, supported by easing energy prices following the de-escalation of US-Iran tensions.
In line with lower crude oil prices, the agency has revised its 2026 Brent crude oil price forecast to around US$80 per barrel from its previous forecast range of US$80 to US$90 per barrel.
The rating agency noted that the ringgit weakened against the US dollar to 4.15 as of June 22 from 3.97 at the start of the month.
This was driven mainly by a stronger greenback, elevated US Treasury yields and expectations of a more hawkish US Federal Reserve.
Foreign portfolio flows also turned negative in May, with net bond outflows of RM4.3 billion compared with net inflows of RM3.8 billion in April.
Net equity outflows amounted to RM3.7 billion against net inflows of RM0.2 billion previously.
MARC expects the ringgit to trade within the 4.00 to 4.15 range against the US dollar, reflecting a wider Malaysian Government Securities-US Treasuries yield differential that continues to favour the greenback.
Nevertheless, Malaysia's strong export performance and sustained foreign direct investment inflows should provide underlying support for the local currency.
On the bond market, Malaysian Government Securities (MGS) yields rose modestly by one to three basis points across the curve as of June 22, with the 10-year benchmark yield closing at 3.61 per cent, compared with 3.59 per cent at end-May.
MARC said the increase largely tracked higher US Treasury yields as markets priced in at least one additional 25-basis-point Federal Reserve rate hike by December.
It added that firmer domestic inflation in May also contributed to the uptick in yields, although the increase remained limited as inflation continued to track within Bank Negara Malaysia's 2026 forecast range of 1.5 per cent to 2.5 per cent.
It expects the 10-year MGS yield to trade within the 3.60 per cent to 3.70 per cent range by year-end, with external factors, particularly hawkish Federal Reserve policy expectations and higher US Treasury yields, remaining the key drivers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DNB says U Mobile exit has no impact, accelerates 5G expansion for AI growth
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Digital Nasional Bhd (DNB) said U Mobile Sdn Bhd's exit from its 5G network on June 30 under the government's dual-network framework will not affect network operations or service continuity.
The state-backed 5G infrastructure provider is now shifting its focus towards expanding capacity to support rising demand for artificial intelligence (AI) and enterprise digital services.
DNB chief executive Datuk Azman Ismail said the company is entering a new phase focused on stronger capacity, collaboration and execution to support growing demand for AI-driven and enterprise digital services.
The company is strengthening Malaysia's 5G infrastructure with additional mid-band spectrum to support growing consumer data usage and rising demand for artificial intelligence (AI) and enterprise digital applications.
DNB has begun activating the 3.3 gigahertz (GHz) to 3.4 GHz spectrum, known as F-Zero. Combined with its existing 3.4GHz to 3.5GHz allocation, DNB now holds 200 megahertz of contiguous mid-band spectrum, the largest such holding in Malaysia.
The additional spectrum effectively doubles the network's capacity, improving efficiency and user experience, particularly in high-traffic areas, with more than 50 sites being upgraded, with further rollouts planned.
In a statement, DNB said the additional capacity will support enterprise digitalisation use cases such as AI, automation, and mission-critical services.
Malaysia's average mobile data usage has doubled from about 19 gigabytes (GB) during the Covid-19 period to around 40GB per user today, while 5G adoption has surpassed 88 per cent or over 30.3 million subscriptions.
DNB said it continues to invest in 5G-advanced capabilities and AI-driven network management while strengthening security and reliability.
The company is also working with shareholders and partners to expand nationwide coverage in support of Malaysia's digital economy goals.
Its existing shareholders, CelcomDigi, Maxis and YTL Communications, collectively serve around 30 million users and have invested more than RM2 billion to date.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: JS-SEZ on track to create 20,000 jobs in three years, ahead of schedule
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) is on track to create 20,000 skilled jobs within three years, ahead of its original five-year timeline.
Economy Minister Datuk Seri Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the government is confident of surpassing the initial target.
"We have to ensure it really translates into quality jobs as well as in terms of the quality of investment.
"I think in terms of the 20,000 target, it can be easily achieved in three years," he told reporters after the JS-SEZ Executive Forum here today.
Akmal also said the JS-SEZ blueprint has been completed and approved by the Cabinet.
"It has actually been completed in terms of documents and also received the approval of the Cabinet. It also has to be highlighted at a larger level to involve the two highest leaders of the country.
"It will be officially launched by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Singapore Prime Minister Lawrence Wong," he said.
He added that the blueprint remains on track for an official launch by the fourth quarter of this year, with implementation frameworks and agencies already working to deliver pledged investments.
The blueprint was initially scheduled for launch on March 30 in Johor Baru.
The JS-SEZ is a strategic cooperation initiative between Malaysia and Singapore aimed at strengthening economic integration, attracting new investments, and stimulating growth in the southern region.
The economic zone aims to transform Johor's economic landscape and achieve a gross domestic product of RM260 billion by 2030.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rapid KL says melted wire triggered Kelana Jaya Line incident
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Rapid KL said a "small explosion" sound was reported on the Kelana Jaya Line yesterday evening involving a train at Taman Jaya station.
The incident occurred at 7.18pm when train No. 57 was travelling towards Putra Heights.
As a safety precaution, the train was withdrawn from service and all passengers were safely disembarked.
No injuries were reported and services on the line continued to operate as normal.
"As a safety measure, the train involved was immediately withdrawn from service and all passengers were safely disembarked at the next station before continuing their journey on another train," Rapid KL said in a statement.
Initial checks by technical teams found no damage to the train or its components, while checks along the track between Universiti and Taman Jaya stations confirmed the infrastructure remained in good condition.
Further inspections were carried out after operational hours as part of standard safety procedures.
Examinations later found that a thin wire, which was not part of the train or track components, had melted at the site and was believed to have come into contact with the power supply system, triggering the incident.
"Investigations found a thin wire, not part of the train or track components, which had melted at the location and was believed to have come into contact with the power supply system, causing the incident," it said.
Rapid KL said it takes all safety-related incidents seriously and would continue investigations while implementing preventive measures to avoid a recurrence.
Passengers have been advised to refer to Rapid KL's official social media channels or the MyRapid PULSE application for the latest updates.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: World Cup betting lands five friends RM3,000 fines
Article:
BUTTERWORTH: Two siblings were among five friends fined RM3,000 each by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to online football betting charges.
Lee Boon Luun, 34, Ooi Wooi Loon, 41, Weeson Lim, 30, and brothers Lau Chee Boon, 30, and Lau Chee Ewe, 28, admitted to the offence when the charge was separately read before magistrate Aini Adilah Muhammad Faizal.
The court ordered each of them to serve five months' jail in default of payment.
They were charged with betting on football matches through online gambling websites at an apartment unit in Marminton Homes, Lorong Cantik Permai 1, about 10.30pm on June 27.
According to the charge sheets, Lee used the GO2WIN.BET website, while the other four accessed the NOVA88 gambling platform using their respective user IDs.
The offence under Section 6(1) of the Betting Act 1953 carries a fine of up to RM5,000, jail of up to six months, or both, upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Siti Nur Farihin Ahmad urged the court to impose an appropriate sentence as a deterrent.
The five were represented by counsel MS Catherine Joysce from the National Legal Aid Foundation.
Catherine pleaded for a minimal fine, saying this was her clients' first offence.
"Their guilty pleas have also saved the court's time and costs. We hope that can be taken into consideration in mitigation," she told the court.
She said Lee, a salesman earning RM2,400 a month, is single and supports his parents.
Ooi, she said, is unemployed, divorced and has two school-going children.
Lim and the Lau brothers, who are all single, had recently either lost or left their jobs, she added.
Aini Adilah imposed a RM3,000 fine on each accused, with five months' imprisonment in default, and ordered the forfeiture and disposal of the seized exhibits.
All five paid the fines.
The case follows a recent statewide crackdown on illegal football betting during the Fifa World Cup 2026.
Police previously arrested 28 people and dismantled two football betting call centres
under Op Soga XI
.
Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim said 21 raids were carried out across the state between June 11 and June 19 under the operation targeting football betting linked to the tournament.
Police seized RM8,935 in cash, computers, mobile phones, communication devices and other equipment believed to have been used in the online gambling operations.
The operation also led to the dismantling of two call centres involved in football betting on the Fifa World Cup 2026.
Yesterday, the George Town Magistrate's Court
rejected a police remand application
against four people, including a woman, detained in connection with football betting activities.
The three men are foreign nationals.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Securing Malaysia's energy future and resilience
Article:
THERE is something surreal about the way the Hormuz crisis has unfolded. For a brief moment, a reported diplomatic breakthrough between Washington and Teheran suggested that the Gulf was finally de-escalating.
Energy markets interpret it the same way. Shipping firms began preparing to resume normal operations, and many observers concluded that the risk of disruption had passed.
The ceasefire allowed exports to flow again and reassured everyone that a wider crisis might be averted.
However, this confidence did not last. On June 20, before the agreement could even take effect, Teheran renewed the closure of the Strait, citing Israeli attacks and what they described as Washington's mala fide.
A ceasefire and blockade were separated by barely seventy-two hours.
Eighty-four per cent of the crude moving through this chokepoint is bound for Asian markets, with China alone drawing a third of its oil supply this way. This is not a distant European problem.
It is an Asian energy artery, and Malaysia is located within its length.
The June ceasefire between the US and Iran attempted to do several things simultaneously. It ended active hostilities, restarted negotiations over Iran's nuclear programme, and committed Iran to toll-free passage through the strait for at least 60 days.
It also required an immediate halt to the Israeli military operations in Lebanon. The markets responded with a sigh of relief.
Brent crude fell almost 5 per cent to its lowest level since early March, and shipping firms began cautiously sending vessels back through the corridor. After months of paralysis, the world wanted to believe that the worst was over.
So why the reversal? Iran's stated reason was that the ceasefire was being violated.
Israeli strikes continued in southern Lebanon, including attacks that killed at least 16 people, even as the deal called for a halt to operations there. Teheran argued that if the other side did not honour its commitments, it would not either.
The United States flatly denied any breaches. The truth is more complicated. Teheran treat the chokepoint as a pressure valve, a way to remind Washington that Iran still holds leverage while diplomats struggle at the negotiation table.
The closure was less a military operation than a signal, sent at a carefully chosen moment, just as technical talks were due to begin.
Iran wants a better final deal, wants Israel out of Lebanon, and wants to show its domestic audience its resilience. The strait reopens and closes based on political risk assessments, not on promises.
Some might assume that Malaysia does not depend directly on petroleum transported through Hormuz. That is the wrong way to measure exposure.
We live in a single global market, and when the strait is threatened, Brent and gas benchmarks rise everywhere at once. War-risk insurance on tankers, which jumped severalfold in the opening days of the conflict, is folded into the price of every cargo shipment.
For an economy whose exports exceed 60 per cent of output, dearer freight directly erodes competitiveness.
The chain extends beyond the fuel pump. Costlier energy feeds into inflation, which climbed to two percent in May, its highest in nearly two years, even as global prices eased.
The government held subsidised RON95 at RM1.99 a litre through BUDI95, cushioning households through the spring spike, but at a cost of over RM3 billion a month to the treasury at the peak of the crisis.
A subsidy is not a shield against a supply shock, it is a transfer of that shock onto the national budget, and a prolonged emergency would test our fiscal space.
For Malaysia, energy security and economic security are one conversation.
We cannot resolve the quarrel between Teheran and Washington, but we can reduce our vulnerability to it. This begins with the diversification of suppliers, routes, and fuels so that no single corridor can hold the country hostage.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's move to seek alternative crude from Brazil and Canada is a step in the right direction.
We should, however, make arrangements in anticipation of the next crisis before it happens. This means strengthening strategic petroleum reserves and expanding gas storage to buy weeks of buffer rather than days.
Malaysia cannot control what happens in the Strait of Hormuz. What we can decide is how much those waters dictate our prices, budgets, and growth.
* The writer is a senior programme officer at Merdeka Centre, focusing on geopolitics and foreign affairs
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Meet 'Piu Piu', the latest drug seized in RM6.85mil bust
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Two brothers were arrested by the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) on Sunday for allegedly attempting to smuggle drugs worth more than RM6.85 million to a neighbouring country by sea.
The brothers are believed to have been paid between RM16,000 and RM17,000 for each drug consignment.
They were detained at 12.36am while waiting in a small fibreglass boat to rendezvous with a vessel from the neighbouring country about 3.5 nautical miles southeast of Tanjung Piai in Pontian waters.
Johor Maritime acting director Maritime Captain Kama Azri Kamil said a search of the boat uncovered several suspicious packages concealed beneath the skipper's seat under a pile of fishing nets.
"The packages contained drugs suspected to be syabu (methamphetamine) and ecstasy pills.
"We initially received information about a suspicious boat lingering in Malaysian waters and immediately deployed a patrol vessel to the location.
"After tracking and intercepting the boat, MMEA officers discovered the packages," he told a press conference at the Johor Maritime headquarters here today.
The seizure comprised about 88.2kg of syabu and 30,000 ecstasy pills.
Kama Azri said the suspects, the 26-year-old skipper and his 40-year-old brother, were arrested at the scene.
Following their arrest, the suspects led MMEA personnel to their home in Gelang Patah, Iskandar Puteri, where officers searched the 26-year-old suspect's car.
Inside the vehicle, officers found 787 vape cartridges suspected to contain a new synthetic drug known as "Piu Piu", estimated to be worth RM196,750.
The substance is believed to be a mixture of psychoactive chemicals and highly toxic opioids, including fentanyl.
"Piu Piu", which means "floating" in Chinese, is a name used by drug syndicates to market the synthetic-laced vape liquid, which purportedly gives users a "light" or "floating" high.
The substance is known to cause severe intoxication, often leaving users paralysed or in a zombie-like state.
Kama Azri said the drugs and the car were seized to assist in the investigation.
He said the brothers' modus operandi was to use small boats to transport drugs from Malaysia to a neighbouring country via maritime routes.
Investigations indicated that this was not their first smuggling attempt and that they were believed to have committed similar offences previously.
"The seized drugs could have fed the habits of more than one million addicts.
"The drugs seized are worth more than RM6.85 million, while the total value of assets seized, including the fibreglass boat used in the smuggling attempt, is estimated at RM90,000.
"This is the largest drug seizure in Johor this year," he said.
Kama Azri said both suspects had been remanded for seven days to facilitate investigations under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952.
"Initial urine screening also confirmed that both suspects tested positive for methamphetamine," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Greenhorn PH candidate unfazed by challenge in Sri Medan
Article:
BATU PAHAT: Pakatan Harapan candidate Hishamuddin @ Misrin Ishak may be a greenhorn, but he will not let that stand in the way of winning support in the Sri Medan constituency in the Johor election.
Sri Medan has long been regarded as a Barisan Nasional stronghold.
Hishamuddin said his confidence is driven by the positive reception from voters towards the Unity Government agenda led by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
"Win or lose, we will do our best. I am not concerned about who our opponent is.
"We hope the leadership of the Prime Minister, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, will resonate in the villages here, and that his struggle will be understood by the people," he said when met during a campaign walkabout in Parit Jalil here.
Commenting on the campaign progress, Hishamuddin, who is also Parit Sulong PKR branch chief, said the response so far has given him confidence to continue engaging voters regardless of political background.
"So far, Alhamdulillah, we have even entered the opponent's stronghold, and the response has been encouraging.
"We will continue our efforts to reach out to them because what matters most is that the people understand what we want to do for Sri Medan," he said.
He also said that among his priorities if given the mandate is upgrading public infrastructure in the constituency.
"To improve basic facilities in Sri Medan, among them is the establishment of a fire station and the upgrading of the old police station so it is on par with other areas.
"We want the people here to enjoy better facilities and balanced development with other areas," he said.
In the Johor election on July 11, Hishamuddin will face incumbent BN candidate Datuk Zulkurnain Kamisan, as well as Perikatan Nasional candidate Ahmad Rosdi Bahari.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Post-mortem confirms missing autistic boy died by drowning
Article:
BAGAN DATUK: A post-mortem has confirmed that a six-year-old autistic boy found dead in a drain near his home in Sungai Sumun, Hutan Melintang here yesterday died from drowning.
District police chief Superintendent Mohammad Ali Mohd Jali said the forensic examination at Hospital Teluk Intan found no signs of physical injury or foul play.
The boy was
reported missing
at 4.54pm yesterday from his family home in Kampung Baru Sungai Sumun.
"Initial investigations revealed that the child, who had autism, was last seen by his father in the living room.
"When the family realised he was missing, they searched the area with residents but could not locate him before lodging a police report," Ali said in a statement today.
Police then set up a control post and launched a search and rescue operation involving the Hutan Melintang Fire and Rescue Department, the Bagan Datuk Civil Defence Force, the Perak police contingent's K9 Unit and villagers.
"At 10pm the same day, the child was found floating in a drain about 330m from his home. He was fully clothed when personnel recovered his body," he said.
He said the case has been classified as sudden death.
"We extend our condolences to the boy's family and express appreciation to all agencies and members of the public who assisted in the search and rescue operation.
"We also urge the public to refrain from speculating or spreading unverified information about the incident out of respect for the victim's family," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: JS-SEZ draws strong interest from Italy, Europe
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) has drawn strong interest from Italy and other European nations looking to deepen bilateral cooperation and secure high-quality investments.
Economy Minister Datuk Seri Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the government briefed a visiting delegation on the progress of the JS-SEZ and the wider investment opportunities available amid global economic uncertainty.
"Today, we have received quite strong interest from Italy, in particular, and also from European countries, to hold a session with potential investors and their chambers," he said at the JS-SEZ Executive Forum here today.
Akmal said the session provided an avenue to outline the development of the economic zone and discuss strategies to build long-term investor confidence.
The forum was attended by Italy's ambassador, Raffaele Langella, and his delegation, alongside representatives from several European countries.
Langella said the visit could mark a milestone in bilateral ties, noting that Italy is looking beyond broad investment pledges toward projects that strengthen wider industrial ecosystems.
"What we are looking for is the quality of the projects that will be set up. Italian companies want to be part of a broader value chain rather than simply making investments in general," he said.
He said Italy hopes to replicate its success in Vietnam's special economic zone, which successfully expanded its commercial footprint in the region.
Langella added that Italian companies already operating in Johor place a strong emphasis on training and education, calling it a practice that should be encouraged in modern joint ventures.
"So we are coming to Johor very soon. We'll bring new potential investors," he said, adding that Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's visit to Rome in July 2025 had accelerated Italian interest in Malaysia.
The JS-SEZ is a joint initiative between Malaysia and Singapore aimed at driving economic integration, attracting new investments, and stimulating growth in the southern region.
The economic zone aims to transform Johor's economy and help the state achieve a gross domestic product of RM260 billion by 2030.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: DAP claim Johor BN is shaky unfounded, says Ahmad Maslan
Article:
BATU PAHAT: DAP's claim that support for Johor Barisan Nasional is wavering due to current political developments is unfounded, Johor Umno deputy chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Maslan said.
He said BN has resolved minor issues in a few of the 56 state seats it is contesting in the 16th Johor election.
He said the issue identified in Rengit has been resolved, while developments in Layang-Layang are being closely monitored to ensure smooth campaign operations.
"So far, I see only about three per cent of issues arising, namely in Rengit and Layang-Layang.
"Out of the 56 state seats being contested, the remaining 54 are facing no problems.
"The case in Rengit has been resolved, while in Layang-Layang we continue to monitor the situation and ensure that the BN candidate from MCA secures victory," he said when met during a walkabout session with BN candidate Datuk Zulkurnain Kamisan in Parit Jawa here yesterday.
Ahmad said his confidence was based on his own observations after visiting several state constituencies during the campaign trail.
He said among the areas he had visited were Bukit Kepong, Bukit Pasir, Rengit, Kukup, Benut and Pulai Sebatang, as well as continuing visits to Maharani, Simpang Jeram, Bukit Naning and Sri Medan.
He urged the BN machinery to continue intensifying efforts to reach voters until the end of the campaign period.
"Continue moving and meeting as many voters as possible. Grassroots efforts must be sustained to ensure the party's message is effectively delivered," he said.
DAP strategic director Liew Chin Tong was quoted as claiming that Johor BN is now in a weakened position due to current political developments in the state, making it difficult for the party to secure more than 40 seats in the July 11 polls.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Xi vows to strengthen China's military, stamp out corruption
Article:
BEIJING: Chinese President Xi Jinping vowed on Wednesday to strengthen and modernise the military and to stamp out corruption within the ruling Communist Party.
Xi underscored the importance of a "strong military" in a speech to political and military leaders as well as Chinese Communist Party members gathered at the Great Hall of the People for the party's 105th founding anniversary.
Analysts have questioned the ability of China's armed forces to fight effectively since Xi's sweeping anti-graft campaign gutted its top ranks.
Xi's years-long effort to purge corruption has brought down two defence ministers in the past three years and reduced the once seven-member Central Military Commission – China's top military body – to just himself and one other general.
The Chinese leader on Wednesday swore to "uphold the party's absolute leadership" over the armed forces and strengthen the military.
"We must advance the modernisation of national defence and the armed forces," Xi told party members.
The CCP also has to "resolutely wage the critical, protracted, and comprehensive battle against corruption", he added.
The Central Military Commission – which Xi heads – has published new measures that would "establish iron rules for strict education, strict management, and strict supervision" of senior military cadres, state media reported in May.
Xi also reiterated on Wednesday his commitment to see China gain control of Taiwan, the self-ruled island Beijing claims as its territory.
"Resolving the Taiwan question and realising the complete unification of the motherland is an unswerving historical mission of our party and the shared aspiration of all Chinese people," he said.
Xi urged his country to "oppose interference by external forces", without naming any specific countries.
The CCP was founded on July 1, 1921 and had more than 101 million members as of late 2025, according to state news agency Xinhua.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bukit Aman: Highway drug crackdown nets 135 drivers, with commercial motorists making up nearly 90pc
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — A total of 135 people were arrested for driving under the influence of drugs between April and June, with nearly 90 per cent of those arrested being commercial vehicle drivers, federal traffic police said today.
Bukit Aman Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) director Commissioner Datuk Seri Muhammed Hasbullah Ali said the inter-agency operations targeting commercial vehicles were launched in response to a noticeable rise in drug use among drivers, prompting the ongoing enforcement initiative.
“JSPT takes a serious view of drug use among commercial vehicle drivers, as it can impair concentration, judgment and vehicle control, significantly increasing the risk of fatal road accidents.
“No compromise will be given to drivers who are found to be driving under the influence of drug as such an act not only endangers oneself but also other road users,” he told a press conference at JSPT headquarters in Bukit Aman here.
The inter-agency operation, involving the Narcotics Crime Investigation Department (NCID) and the National Anti-Drug Agency (AADK), saw a total of 44 operations carried out by 627 enforcement officers along the PLUS Expressway and the East Coast Expressway.
Of the 135 individuals arrested, 121 were commercial vehicle drivers, while the remaining 14 were drivers of non-commercial vehicles.
Muhammed Hasbullah said most of the substances detected were amphetamines, reportedly used to boost concentration during long hours of driving.
“JSPT also reminds logistics companies to conduct periodic monitoring of their drivers, including drug screening, and to adopt a non-compromising approach towards any employee found to be involved in drug abuse,” he said.

Title: Pakatan’s Sharon Teo rejects ‘parachute candidate’ label, cites years under Salahuddin’s mentorship
Article:
JOHOR BHARU, July 1 — Her experience serving as a special officer to the late Datuk Seri Salahuddin Ayub has shaped the political values and approach adopted by Sharon Teo Siew Hui, the Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for Permas, who has pledged to place public service at the centre of her agenda if given the mandate in the Johor State Election.
Teo, 36, said she gained invaluable leadership lessons from the late Minister of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living, particularly in humility, accessibility and a steadfast commitment to serving the people regardless of race or background.
She described the late leader, widely associated with the title “Bapa Rahmah Malaysia”, as the primary inspiration behind her political journey.
“I joined Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) in 2018 because he was my mentor. Before that, I only supported him voluntarily, but after getting to know him better, I was inspired by his character and the way he served the people.
“He was a very humble person. That is the spirit I want to bring to Permas,” she said in an interview with Bernama today.
Teo said accompanying the late Salahuddin throughout several general elections and state elections gave her a deeper understanding that being an elected representative goes beyond receiving complaints—it requires ensuring issues are followed through until they are resolved.
“I still remember how he personally monitored the progress of public complaints until late at night. Sometimes, even close to midnight, he would send WhatsApp messages asking whether people’s problems had been resolved.
“That is the approach I want to bring to Permas,” she said.
Responding to claims that she was a “parachute candidate”, Teo dismissed the label, saying it did not reflect her political journey.
She said she has been actively involved with Amanah since 2018, beginning as an ordinary member before serving as Assistant Secretary of Amanah Johor and later becoming head of Amanah Johor Wanita Muda (WARDA).
Teo added that Permas is also familiar territory, as she frequently accompanied the late Salahuddin on campaign visits and community engagements in the area during previous elections.
She said the reception from voters during the first five days of campaigning had strengthened her confidence to continue engaging residents from various backgrounds.
Among the issues most frequently raised by voters, she said, were infrastructure concerns including potholes, deteriorating back lanes behind commercial premises, traffic congestion and the need to upgrade public facilities.
Teo also pledged to focus on young people, especially first-time voters and school leavers, by engaging them through social media outreach and e-sports initiatives.
“If elected, she said her first 100 days in office would focus on identifying the most urgent issues affecting the Permas constituency, gathering comprehensive data and developing phased solutions.
“My three main priorities are to establish PermasKu as a one-stop centre for managing public complaints so that every issue is monitored until resolution, and conduct a comprehensive infrastructure audit across Permas to identify urgent priorities.
“I will also go directly to every area to engage residents and develop action plans based on real community needs rather than assumptions,” she said.
The Permas state seat will see a four-cornered contest involving Teo, the incumbent and Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Baharudin Mohamed Taib, Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) candidate Dr Zamil Najwah and Perikatan Nasional (PN) candidate T. Vela.
For the latest updates on the 16th Johor State Election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor/. — Bernama

Title: Court adjourns Rosmah’s solar hybrid graft appeal to September 1, says no more delays
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — The Court of Appeal in Putrajaya today adjourned Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor’s appeal against her conviction in the RM1.25 billion solar hybrid project corruption case until September, adding that it would not allow any further adjournments.
According to
The Star
, a three-member bench chaired by Datuk Azhahari Kamal Ramli allowed the hearing, originally scheduled to begin today, to be postponed pending the Federal Court’s decision on Rosmah’s review application linked to her failed bid to recuse the trial judge who convicted her, Datuk Mohamed Zaini Mazlan.
The Federal Court is scheduled to hear the review application on July 29.
In allowing the adjournment, Justice Azhahari said the court had taken note of a letter dated October 10, 2024, from the president of the Court of Appeal instructing that the main appeal be deferred until the review application had been disposed of.
He also described the matter as “an ageing case”, noting that it dates back to 2022.
“We have fixed the appeal for hearing. We will not allow any further adjournment,” he said.
The appeal is now scheduled to be heard from September 1 to 4, September 8 to 11, and September 21 to 30.
Justices Datuk Choo Kah Sing and K. Muniandy sat with Justice Azhahari on the panel.
The court also took into account the personal circumstances of one of Rosmah’s lawyers, Datuk Akberdin Abdul Kader, whose son died in a road crash in May.
During proceedings, co-counsel Datuk Jagjit Singh teared up as he told the court that Akberdin was still grieving the loss of his 24-year-old son, Adli Hakim, who was among three people killed in a collision with a cement lorry on the Karak Highway on May 22.
Jagjit said Akberdin had prepared a substantial portion of the written submissions and that his presence was important for the appeal.
“He (Akberdin) is like my own younger brother. He is still grieving and mourning. We hope the prosecution can understand this situation,” Jagjit told the court.
Deputy public prosecutor K. Mangai said the prosecution had been instructed to oppose the adjournment because the appeal had been pending before the Court of Appeal since 2023, but left the matter to the court’s discretion in light of Akberdin’s bereavement.
The adjournment follows the Federal Court’s decision in March dismissing Rosmah’s bid to recuse Justice Mohamed Zaini and order a retrial, ruling there was no appealable error warranting its intervention. Rosmah subsequently filed a review application against that decision, which the Court of Appeal said should be resolved before the appeal proceeds.
Rosmah sought to recuse Justice Mohamed Zaini after a draft judgment in her case was leaked before he delivered his decision in September 2022. She argued that the draft had been prepared by a third party, but her application was dismissed by the High Court, the Court of Appeal and later the Federal Court.
Justice Mohamed Zaini convicted Rosmah on September 1, 2022, sentencing her to 10 years’ imprisonment and a RM970 million fine after finding her guilty over the Sarawak solar hybrid project.
She was charged with one count of soliciting RM187.5 million and two counts of receiving RM6.5 million in bribes from former Jepak Holdings Sdn Bhd managing director Saidi Abang Samsudin in connection with the project.
Rosmah remains free on a stay of execution pending the outcome of her appeal.

Title: No Haj package scams reported this season, says religious affairs minister
Article:
SEPANG, 1 July — No cases of Haj and Badal Haj (Haj by proxy) package scams were recorded throughout the 1447H/2026M season following integrated monitoring by various agencies, said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan.
He attributed the success to close cooperation between Tabung Haji (TH), the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and relevant authorities, who conducted physical monitoring at the Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) alongside digital surveillance on social media platforms.
“Alhamdulillah, we received no reports of fraud, including Badal Haj scams. This demonstrates the effectiveness of our measures and we are grateful for achieving ‘zero’ scammer cases this year,” he told reporters after welcoming the final flight of Haj pilgrims here today.
Also present were Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Marhamah Rosli and TH Chairman Tan Sri Abdul Rashid Hussain.
Meanwhile, Zulkifli noted that TH successfully reduced the deferment rate for Haj offers from 50 per cent last year to 18 per cent this season, following proactive steps such as early notifications to prospective pilgrims and continuous preparation campaigns.
“We launched the #menujuistitoah campaign last February to encourage early preparation, with Haj offers finalised by Ramadan 1447H and significantly earlier than in previous seasons,” he added.
The final flight from the Holy Land saw 258 pilgrims arrive at KLIA at 12.10pm aboard Malaysia Airlines flight MH 8385, which had departed Madinah at 10.14pm (Saudi Arabian time) yesterday. — Bernama

Title: Thailand, Malaysia eye September peace talks on southern border conflict
Article:
BANGKOK, July 1 — Thailand and Malaysia are expected to hold high-level peace talks on the situation in Thailand’s southern border provinces in September, with both sides aiming to reach an agreement that could pave the way for a joint declaration.
Wan Muhamad Noor Matha, who serves as Political Adviser to the Thai Prime Minister and adviser to the government’s Special Representative Delegation, said preliminary discussions are currently being conducted by a subcommittee behind closed doors.
“There are about two more subcommittee meetings to be held before the main committee convenes in Malaysia in September.
“If the main committee reaches an agreement, both sides may sign a joint document and issue a statement reflecting the agreed outcomes,” he told reporters at Government House today.
Wan Muhamad Noor expressed optimism that the peace dialogue was progressing in a positive direction.
On the expected timeline for the peace process, he said the outcome of the September meeting would provide a clearer indication, adding that he anticipated considerably more progress than in previous rounds of talks.
Meanwhile, on Prime Minister and Interior Minister Anutin Charnvirakul’s planned official visit to Malaysia on July 9-10, Wan Muhamad Noor said the visit was primarily aimed at strengthening bilateral ties and cooperation within ASEAN.
